page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Myelodysplastic Syndrome: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Myelodysplastic Syndrome: 178 alterations and 576 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: 6-shogaol caused a decrease in serum ferritin levels in 3 of 6 patients with early Myelodysplastic syndrome.
A maitake mushroom extract has beneficial immunomodulatory potential in patients with myelodysplastic syndromes.
Coenzyme Q10 may be of clinical benefit in a subset of myelodysplastic syndromes.
Exposure to various chemicals is associated with increased risk for myelodysplastic syndrome according to the WHO.
l-Ascorbic acid may be of clinical benefit in Acute Myeloid Leukemia and Myelodysplastic Syndromes.
The Chinese Herbal Formula Qinghuang is therapeutic in the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome.
The Chinese Herbal formula Yisuikang is effective in treating some forms of myelodysplastic syndromes.
The study suggests that regular tea consumption may reduce the risk of de novo myelodysplastic syndromes.
Therapy-related acute myeloid leukemia and myelodysplastic syndrome has been reported.

There is an increased incidence of myelodysplastic syndrome and acute myeloid leukemia following breast cancer treatment with radiation alone or combined with chemotherapy: a registry cohort analysis 1990-2005.
Vitamin D may be therapeutic in the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome.
Vitamin E and a synthetic analogue of vitamin A improve the treatment outcome of myelodysplastic syndrome.
Vitamin K2 appears effective in the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome - Article 1.
Vitamin K2 appears effective in the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome - Article 2
Vitamin K2 plus Vitamin D3 are therapeutic for the treatment of anemia and thyrombocytopenia associated wtih low/intermediate myelodysplastic syndrome.
A case of hypersensitivity reaction, myelodysplastic syndrome and pancytopenia, which developed after an thimerosal-containing tetanus vaccine.
A case report of vitamin B12 deficiency mimicking acute leukemia.
Anti-cancer effects of curcumin on myelodysplastic syndrome through the inhibition of enhancer of zeste homolog-2 (EZH2).
Cat's claw (Uncaria tomentosa) increases the number of myeloid progenitor cells and the level of colony-stimulating factor in Listeria infected mice.
Curcumin as an epigenetic therapeutic agent in myelodysplastic syndromes.
Curcumin may represent a new horizon in managing iron overload-induced toxicity as well as in pathological diseases characterized by hepatic iron accumulation.
Euphorbia tirucalli has a myelopoietic effect which may contribute to its anti-tumor activity in mice.
Woodfordia fructicosa has an immunostimulatory effect and may protect the bone marrow.
Astragalus stimulates hematopoiesis and may attenuate the myelosuppressive effects of anti-cancer chemotherapy.
Polydatin exerts therapeutic effects on myelodysplastic syndrome.
Triptolide inhibits transcription factor NF-kappa B, which translates into a therapeutic effect against various leukemias, multiple myeloma and myelodysplastic syndrome.
Wogonin inhibits the proliferation of myelodysplastic syndrome cells.
Copper deficiency may be implicated in Myelodysplastic syndrome.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Essentially zero; progressive cytopaenia and transformation to AML in 30-40% of higher-risk MDS over 2-5 years; median survival low-risk MDS ~5 years, high-risk ~1 year without treatment
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Allogeneic HSCT: only curative option; 5-year OS 35-50% in fit patients; hypomethylating agents (azacitidine, decitabine): ORR 15-17%, overall survival improvement 9 months vs SC; oral azacitidine (CC-486): 10.7 vs 8.9 months OS in AML maintenance post-remission
remission.gap_size: Large — MDS has limited treatment options; curative HSCT used in <15% of patients (age, comorbidity, donor availability); existing drugs are not curative
remission.barrier_detail: MDS predominantly affects elderly patients who are often HSCT-ineligible; hypomethylating agents provide modest benefit without cure; luspatercept (TGF-β trap) approved for LR-MDS with ring sideroblasts 2020 — improved transfusion independence; imetelstat for LR-MDS — Phase 3 (IMerge) showed independence from transfusion in 40% vs 15% placebo
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — imetelstat (telomerase inhibitor) FDA approved for LR-MDS; luspatercept (2020) for RS+ LR-MDS; oral azacitidine (2020); parenteral azacitidine since 2004; EPO since mid-1990s
remission.outdated_treatments: Danazol (androgens) for anaemia in LR-MDS (variable response, side effects; superseded by luspatercept/EPO); erythropoiesis-stimulating agents at high ESA doses without luspatercept or imetelstat consideration; watch-and-wait without transfusion thresholds or iron chelation monitoring
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Iron chelation with deferasirox in transfusion-dependent MDS (reduces iron overload, emerging OS data); oral luspatercept in clinical practice optimisation; exercise programme to maintain functional status in elderly MDS patients
remission.frontier_signal: Imetelstat (first-in-class telomerase inhibitor — phase 3 positive 2023); sabatolimab (anti-TIM-3 immune checkpoint — Phase 3 ongoing); magrolimab (anti-CD47) + azacitidine; oral HMAs (novel decitabine formulations); venetoclax + azacitidine extension to MDS (VERMONT trial)
